executive_directors_report: Courtesy Officers: Two courtesy officers have been chosen to work at Attick Towers.

Budget items: Cameras will be installed (in front of building, laundry room, and blind spots), physical needs assessment projects, accountant putting together salaries, additional positions, repairs of first floor, repairs denied by insurance company because of delayed submission of claims, and Board suggestions of further budget items.
Parking: The Board reviewed/discussed a spread sheet that showed cost that were incurred by the HACCP from 2018 — 2019.
Free meals to residents: The Senior Service Nutrition Program of P.G. County will be suppling residents at Attick Towers with frozen meals twice a month until December.
RFP Legal Services: RFP’s are being solicited to be obtained by the HACCP.
T-Mobile: The contract with T-Mobile is put on hold until further notice.
Retreat: The Board discussed the importance of having a retreat, and the various ways, i.e. (in person or remotely through zoom).
decisions: The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 to approve the financial report, as submitted.
The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 to approve the regular monthly meeting minutes held on June 25, 2020, as amended.

The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 to approve the renewal of the construction permit, and instruct that Ms. Johnson draw up a letter of the Boards intent to move forward in requesting, that the parcel of land the City agreed upon, the HACCP could utilize and install twelve parking spaces.
The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 to approve the payment of the invoices submitted over $2,000.
The motion was unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0 to adjourn the regular monthly meeting.

attendees: Attick Towers Property Manager, Cheryl Gleason
Guest, Water Savings, Inc., Mike Reible
residents_comments: None.
Residents of Attick Towers liked the new Board meeting entrance door.
executive_directors_report: Water Savings Presentation — Mike Reible — Water Savings, Inc.:
- Water Savings, Inc. has been in business for 26 years
- Reviewed water savings for current and previous clients
- Reviewed water bills at Attick Towers for errors (none were discovered)
- Water usage is very high at Attick Towers
- Site inspection performed at Attick Towers (qualified for shared savings program)
- Goal to bring water bill down 32%
- Replace toilets, shower heads, and rebuild faucets
Obsolescence Update:
- Board reviewed visual obsolescence update material
- HUD requirement is 62% - Attick Towers scored 69% - Goal is 75%
- Suggested to reach the goal by doing a Geo Tech review, Structural engineer review, and Soil boring study
ROSS Grant Overview:
- HACCP received the FY20 ROSS grant
- Grant funds to be utilized for salary of services coordinator, training, training travel expenses, administrative costs, and office equipment and space for coordinator, etc.
decisions: Motion to approve the agenda as submitted, unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0
Motion to approve the regular monthly meeting minutes from June 24, 2021, unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0
Motion to terminate the contract with Progressive Security Co. and hire Lt. Corbin as a contractor security officer at Attick Towers, unanimously approved by a vote of 5-0
Motion to approve the payment of the invoices over $2,000, unanimously approved by a vote of 4-0

Board Minutes Regular Monthly Meeting Teleconference
July 22, 2021
Present: Chairperson, Arelis Perez; Vice Chairperson, Robert Catlin; Commissioners, Theresa Keeler, James McFadden, and Betsy Lynn; Executive Director, Michelle Johnson; Attick Towers Property Manager, Cheryl Gleason; Guest, Water Savings, Inc., Mike Reible...

residents_comments: None
executive_directors_report: - Repositioning: Tested for asbestos, mold, and performed an evaluation on the electrical, plumbing, and structure of the building.
- Update CDBG: The Board reviewed/discussed the CDBG grant. The HACCP was awarded the grant for $372,752.00.
- Property Updates: Storm damage, several trees came down, tree fell down on the HVAC unit (outside boiler), and the insurance deductible was 1% of $15M.
- Repairing of piping on the first floor (working on for four months)
- Unit 101 - on August 1* will be totally cleaned out because of asbestos.
- Value Assessment of Attick Towers (building and the land)
- Leased two units (one resident passed away, one resident moved out, and fifteen units left to be leased)
- Landscape company took care of fallen trees
- Cameras scheduled to be installed in the lobby
- Costs solicited for cameras to be installed out front of the building
- Pest control
- Gave a presentation of events and resident activities (covid vaccine clinic, national freeze pop day, meals on wheels, resident walks, arts and crafts, and bingo)
- Store bus trips and upcoming events
decisions: - Agenda changes reviewed and approved
- Approved a budget of $2,000 senior prom, to be paid from the resident funds
- Rescinded resident commissioner's formal resignation as the non-profit housing coalition director
- Approved the financial reports of June 30, 2022, as submitted
- Approved the regular monthly meeting minutes from Jun 2, as submitted
- Invoices over $4,000 were unanimously approved.
- The Board adjourned the meeting at July 28, 2022 at 11:16 a.m.

executive_directors_report: 1) RFP Plumbing: The RFP will be available on June 1, 2021 by request.
2) ACOP (Admissions and Continued Occupancy Policy) Resolution: The Board reviewed/discussed the revisions and updates to the ACOP resolution.
3) Parking Project: Ms. Johnson discussed a meeting in March with the City and the proposed utilization of the parcel of land.
4) Communication Structure: Ms. Johnson discussed the structure of communication between the Board, Executive Director and staff.
5) Board Stipend: Ms. Johnson discussed HUD regulations and the ACOP noting that residents, as well as resident commissioners may only receive a stipend payment of $200 or less monthly.
1) Update on MDE water sampling program: The samplings have been consistently done.

residents_comments: Mr. Patterson thanked staff for the positive message that has been presented toward the residents of Attick Towers.
A resident expressed appreciation for the garden clean up.
A resident thanked staff for a positive change in attitude. He also mentioned that the residents' concern is the smoking in the units at Attick Towers.

executive_directors_report: Commissioner training: Ms. Keeler and Ms. Evans signed up for training for July 26 and 27, 2023
Bylaws: There has been no response from the commissioners on dates to discuss the bylaws. Ms. Johnson requested the Board submit 3 dates for training by June 2, 2023.
CBDG grant: The HACCP has been approved to receive the grant of $2 million by the state of MD.
Repositioning plan: HUD representative was Ms. Nicole Whitcliffe. Ms. Whitcliffe is no longer consulting with HUD. The new rep. is on vacation. Follow up with her will be the next step asap.
95 occupied units, 13 are vacant, 3 modernizations off line
French doors installed in the lobby to allow easier access into the activity room by wheelchair.
The HACCP is looking to install solar panels at the property.
Trees coming down and hanging over Attick Towers property.
The Health and Wellness Fair was a success.
None.
One invoice submitted over $4,000 was approved.

Approval of the regular monthly meeting minutes from April 27, 2023

Financials: close out of fiscal year 2020, operating results for the year (due to HUD by November 30th, Cares funds, last year reserves, and estimated reserves (rainy day fund).

Budget: salaries, once a year revision to the budget, minimum completed past budgets, PUM (per unit months), operating receipts per month, administrative expenses per unit months, tenant services, utilities, maintenance/operations, protective services, PILOT, employee contributions, write offs, operating expenses (3 to 6 months), capital improvements and replacements, deficits, HUD recouping overage of funds in reserves, IRS penalties concerning contractor vs. employee designations, rental of office space, rental revenue, maintenance and operational materials, contract costs, back up information for insurance rates, water treatment (cooling tower), Covid expenses, employee benefits, HVAC and plumbing upgrades.
